steps.title: Identify the relationship between the two sentences
steps.content:
The first sentence describes snowpack behavior at lower elevations (deepest in early March). The sentence being completed shifts to higher elevations, which showed a different peak time (mid-March). These are two distinct conditions being compared — the relationship is contrast, not clarification, example, or summary.
  • Choice A — in other words,: signals restatement of the same idea ✗
  • Choice B — for instance,: signals an example of the preceding point ✗
  • Choice C — on the other hand,: signals a contrasting condition ✓
  • Choice D — in summary,: signals a concluding wrap-up, not a comparison ✗

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: Only “on the other hand” marks the shift from lower-elevation behavior to the contrasting higher-elevation behavior. That is the correct answer.
